title: Pinalia abbreviata
taxonomy: Pinalia abbreviata is classified as a species within the genus Pinalia, which is a part of the family Orchidaceae. This classification places it as a type of orchid, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Within the Orchidaceae family, Pinalia abbreviata is further classified as a member of the subfamily Epidendroideae. The exact classification of Pinalia abbreviata within the Orchidaceae family may vary depending on the source, but it is generally recognized as an orchid species. The classification of Pinalia abbreviata is subject to change as new research and discoveries are made.

In traditional medicine, Pinalia abbreviata is sometimes used to treat various ailments, including fever and respiratory issues. intro: Pinalia abbreviata, also known as Short-stemmed Pinalia or Pinalia, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family, characterized by its small size and short stems. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities that may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Pinalia abbreviata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Pinalia abbreviata is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, often used to treat fever, sore throat, and respiratory infections.
